[http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/joe-greenwood/summary.html RLP]{{Rugby league records |id=4291 |title=Joe Greenwood |access-date=24 September 2023}} |updated = 2 April 2025 | module = {{infobox person | embed = yes | father = | mother = | spouse = | education = | alma_mater = | relatives = [[James Greenwood (rugby league)|James Greenwood]] (brother) }} }} '''Joe Greenwood''' (born 2 April 1993) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]],{{Cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/team|title=St Helens Profile|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=2011-04-30|access-date=2011-04-30}} the [[England Knights]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights v Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|access-date=24 October 2019}} He played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] and the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the top flight. Greenwood has also played for the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[NRL]]. ==Background== Greenwood was born in [[Oldham]], [[Greater Manchester]], England. He played his junior rugby league for the [[Saddleworth]] Rangers club. ==Career== ===St Helens=== In 2014 won the Super League Championship with St Helens. ===Gold Coast Titans=== On 20 February 2017, he signed to play for the [[Gold Coast Titans|Gold Coast]] in the [[National Rugby League]].[http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/englishman-joe-greenwood-joins-gold-coast-in-swap-deal-for-zeb-taia/news-story/ab4b70b0ab31e6a73019afcad612fd65 Englishman Joe Greenwood joins Gold Coast in swap deal] ===Wigan Warriors=== He played in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=13 October 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}} ===Leeds Rhinos (loan)=== On 25 February 2020, Wigan allowed Greenwood to sign a short eight-week loan with Leeds.{{cite web | url=https://www.totalrl.com/five-ex-super-league-players-who-bizarrely-never-played-a-game-for-the-club-they-signed-for/ | title=Five ex-Super League players who bizarrely never played a game for the club they signed for | first=Callum | last=Walker | date=24 January 2025 | access-date=25 January 2025 | publisher=Total Rugby League}} On 16 March, the [[Rugby Football League]] announced the [[Impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on rugby league|postponement all rugby league fixtures]] in England due to the [[COVID-19 pandemic]],{{cite news | url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby/rugby-league/coronavirus-super-league-rugby-suspended-postponed-latest-a9405016.html | title=Coronavirus: Super League season suspended in wake of outbreak | work=The Independent | first=Ben | last=Burrows | date=16 March 2020 | access-date=25 January 2025}} and Greenwood returned to Wigan without making an appearance for Leeds when the competition restarted in August.{{cite news | title=Super League confirms return date | url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby/rugby-league/super-league-2020-season-restart-date-confirmed-a9587936.html | access-date=25 January 2025 | work=The Independent | date=26 June 2020 }} ===Wigan Warriors (return)=== He played in the [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] which Wigan lost 8-4 against St Helens.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport }} ===Huddersfield Giants=== On 10 December 2020, it was announced that Greenwood would be joining Huddersfield on a two-year deal from the 2021 season.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55257904|title=Joe Greenwood: Huddersfield sign Wigan forward on two-year contract|work=BBC Sport |access-date=13 Dec 2020}} In round 2 of the 2021 Super League season, he was sent off after a dangerous high tackle in Huddersfield's 20-10 loss against the [[Catalans Dragons]].{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/56577576|title=Huddersfield Giants 10-20 Catalans Dragons - Tom Davies sets Catalans on way to victory|work=BBC Sport }} On 28 May 2022, Greenwood played for Huddersfield in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] loss to [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-player-ratings-final-24089809/|title=Challenge Cup player ratings as numerous stars shine in enthralling contest|website=www.examinerlive.co.uk|access-date=29 May 2022|archive-date=3 June 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220603014903/https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-player-ratings-final-24089809|url-status=dead}} Greenwood played 14 matches with Huddersfield in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished ninth on the table and missed the playoffs.{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ian-watson-explains-why-huddersfield-giants-underperformed-in-super-league-2023/#:~:text=HUDDERSFIELD%20GIANTS%20have%20been%20one,silverware%20once%20more%20this%20season./|title=Ian Watson explains why Huddersfield Giants underperformed in Super League 2023|website=www.totalrl.com|date=29 September 2023 }} Greenwood played 14 games for Huddersfield in the 2024 Super League season as the club finished 9th for a second consecutive year.{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-34-castleford-tigers-10-hosts-win-dead-rubber-but-2024-season-one-to-forget-for-west-yorkshire-rivals-4789784/|title=Huddersfield Giants 34 Castleford Tigers 10: Hosts win dead rubber but 2024 season one to forget for West Yorkshire rivals|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk|date=19 September 2024 }} Greenwood played 23 matches for Huddersfield in the 2025 Super League season as the club finished 10th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/huddersfield-giants-coach-sounds-nrl-warning-as-honest-admission-made-over-2025-shortcomings/|title=Huddersfield Giants coach sounds NRL warning as honest admission made over 2025 shortcomings|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}} ==International career== In July 2018 he was selected in the [[England Knights]] Performance squad.{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=24 July 2018|access-date=31 October 2019}} Greenwood made his full international début v [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at [[Elland Road]], Leeds on 11 November 2018, coming off the bench in the 0-34 defeat.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/dates/11-november-2018/england-vs-new-zealand.html|title=England 0-38 New Zealand|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org|access-date=13 Dec 2020}}
In 2019 he was selected for the [[England Knights]] against [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights vs Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|access-date=30 October 2019}} ==Personal== Joe Greenwood's older brother, [[James Greenwood (rugby league)|James Greenwood]], also plays as a professional.{{citation needed|date=August 2023}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.therhinos.co.uk/2020/02/25/rhinos-sign-wigan-forward-on-two-month-loan/] *[https://www.wiganwarriors.com/teams/first-team/joe-greenwood Wigan Warriors profile] *[https://www.superleague.co.uk/player/416/joe-greenwood SL profile] *[https://www.titans.com.au/teams/telstra-premiership/gold-coast-titans/joe-greenwood/ Gold Coast Titans profile] *[http://www.saintsrlfc.com/team/73 St Helens profile] *[http://www.saints.org.uk/saints/player.php?num=16723 Saints Heritage Society profile] {{Huddersfield Giants squad}} {{Wigan Warriors - 2018 Super League Grand Final winners}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Greenwood, Joe}} [[Category:1993 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:England Knights national rugby league team players]] [[Category:England national rugby league team players]] [[Category:English rugby league players]] [[Category:Gold Coast Titans players]] [[Category:Huddersfield Giants players]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Oldham]] [[Category:Rugby league second-rows]] [[Category:St Helens R.F.C. players]] [[Category:Wigan Warriors players]] [[Category:21st-century English sportsmen]]
